You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: cbee8223).

## Issue to Resolve

Repeat format step until no more errors are found which can be auto-corrected
The order in which rules are executed can be influences with `Rule.VisitorModifier`. Now the network or rule execution dependencies get more complex, it becomes difficult to find that single order of rules which always resolves all format errors in a single trip. This is confusing for users of kltint.

If format finds any lint violations in a file that can be autocorrected, it should automatically rerun format on that file before returning the lint violations of the last format run. To prevent that a set of rules cause an infinite rerun of the format, no more than  3 reruns will be executed before returning the results.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `RELEASE_TESTING.MD`
- `documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ASTNodeExtension.kt`

- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/api/KtLintRuleEngine.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/build.gradle.kts`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ArgumentListWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BinaryExpressionWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BlankLineBeforeDeclarationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IfElseWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

102→

104→        val errors = mutableListOf<LintError>()

112→                }
113→            }
114→
115→        errors
116→            .sortedWith { l, r -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
117→            .forEach { e -> callback(e) }

120→    }

123→     * Fix style violations in [code] for lint errors when possible. If [code] is passed as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on
124→     * the path are taken into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.

126→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
127→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation

130→        code: Code,
131→        callback: (LintError, Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _ -> },
132→    ): String {
133→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
134→
135→        val hasUTF8BOM = code.content.startsWith(UTF8_BOM)
136→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
137→
138→        var tripped = false
139→        var mutated = false
140→        val errors = mutableSetOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
141→        val visitorProvider = VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
142→        visitorProvider
143→            .visitor()
144→            .invoke { rule ->
145→                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
146→                    tripped = true
147→                    if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
148→                        mutated = true
149→                        /**
150→                         * Rebuild the suppression locator after each change in the AST as the offsets of the
151→                         * suppression hints might have changed.
152→                         */
153→                        ruleExecutionContext.rebuildSuppressionLocator()
154→                    }
155→                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
156→                    errors.add(
157→                        Pair(
158→                            LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
159→                            // It is assumed that a rule that emits that an error can be autocorrected, also
160→                            // does correct the error.
161→                            canBeAutoCorrected,
162→                        ),
163→                    )

166→                    // violations are being solved.
167→                    LOGGER.trace {

170→                    }

172→            }
173→        if (tripped) {
174→            visitorProvider
175→                .visitor()
176→                .invoke { rule ->
177→                    ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(
178→                        rule,
179→                        false,
180→                    ) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
181→                        val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
182→                        errors.add(
183→                            Pair(
184→                                LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
185→                                // It is assumed that a rule only corrects an error after it has emitted an
186→                                // error and indicating that it actually can be autocorrected.
187→                                false,
188→                            ),
189→                        )
190→                    }
191→                }
192→        }
193→
194→        errors
195→            .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
196→            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
197→
198→        if (!mutated) {
199→            return code.content
200→        }
201→
202→        val formattedCode =
203→            ruleExecutionContext
204→                .rootNode
205→                .text
206→                .replace("\n", ruleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(code.content))
207→        return if (hasUTF8BOM) {
208→            UTF8_BOM + formattedCode
209→        } else {
210→            formattedCode
211→        }.also {
212→            LOGGER.debug("Finished with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'")
213→        }
214→    }

216→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
217→        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
218→        return when {
219→            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
220→            else -> "\n"
221→        }
222→    }

224→    /**
225→     * Generates Kotlin `.editorconfig` file section content based on a path to a file or directory. Given that path, all '.editorconfig'
226→     * files on that path are taken into account to determine the values of properties which are already used.
227→     *
228→     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
229→     * ```properties
230→     * final-newline=true
231→     * indent-size=4
232→     * ```
233→     */
234→    public fun generateKotlinEditorConfigSection(filePath: Path): String {
235→        val codeStyle =
236→            editorConfigOverride
237→                .properties[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
238→                ?.parsed
239→                ?.safeAs<CodeStyleValue>()
240→                ?: CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
241→        val rules =

244→                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
245→                .toSet()

248→                rules,

252→    }

 28→
 29→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 30→    val code: Code,
 31→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 32→    val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
 33→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 34→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 35→) {
 36→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 37→
 38→    init {
 39→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 43→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode, editorConfig)
 44→    }
 45→
 46→    fun executeRule(
 47→        rule: Rule,
 48→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 49→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 50→    ) {
 51→        try {
 52→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 53→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 54→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 55→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 56→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 57→                // Note that also the CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY is provided because that property is needed to determine the default value of an
 58→                // EditorConfigProperty that is not explicitly defined.
 59→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties.plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY)),
 60→            )
 61→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, autoCorrect, emit)
 62→            rule.afterLastNode()
 63→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 64→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 65→                e.line,
 66→                e.col,
 67→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 68→                """
 69→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 70→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 71→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 72→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 73→                """.trimIndent(),
 74→                e.cause,
 75→            )
 76→        }
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 80→        node: ASTNode,
 81→        rule: Rule,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        /**
 86→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 87→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 88→         */
 89→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 91→            try {
 92→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 93→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→                }
 95→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 96→                    node
 97→                        .getChildren(null)
 98→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 99→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
100→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
101→                                    childNode,
102→                                    rule,
103→                                    autoCorrect,
104→                                    emit,
105→                                )
106→                            }
107→                        }
108→                }
109→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
110→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
111→                }
112→            } catch (e: Exception) {
113→                if (autoCorrect) {
114→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
115→                    // original AST
116→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
117→                        rule,
118→                        0,
119→                        0,
120→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
121→                        e.cause ?: e,
122→                    )
123→                } else {
124→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
125→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
126→                        rule,
127→                        line,
128→                        col,
129→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
130→                        e.cause ?: e,
131→                    )
132→                }
133→            }
134→        }
135→    }

137→    companion object {
138→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
139→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
140→            code: Code,
141→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
142→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine)
143→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
144→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
145→
146→            val psiFileName =
147→                code
148→                    .filePath
149→                    ?.pathString
150→                    ?: if (code.script) {
151→                        "File.kts"
152→                    } else {
153→                        "File.kt"
154→                    }
155→            val psiFile =
156→                psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
157→                    psiFileName,
158→                    KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
159→                    normalizedText,
160→                ) as KtFile
161→            psiFile
162→                .findErrorElement()
163→                ?.let { errorElement ->
164→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
165→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
166→                }
167→
168→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
169→
170→            val editorConfig =
171→                ktLintRuleEngine
172→                    .editorConfigLoader
173→                    .load(code.filePath)
174→                    .also {
175→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
176→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
177→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
178→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
179→                    }
180→
181→            val ruleProviders =
182→                ktLintRuleEngine
183→                    .ruleProviders(
184→                        RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
185→                        RunAfterRuleFilter(),
186→                    )
187→
188→            return RuleExecutionContext(
189→                code,
190→                rootNode,
191→                ruleProviders,
192→                editorConfig,
193→                positionInTextLocator,
194→            )
195→        }
196→
197→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
198→            return text
199→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
200→                .replace("\r", "\n")
201→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
202→        }
203→
204→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
205→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
206→                return this
207→            }
208→            this.children.forEach { child ->
209→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
210→                if (errorElement != null) {
211→                    return errorElement
212→                }
213→            }
214→            return null
215→        }
216→    }
217→}

219→private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
220→    propertyName: String,
221→    ktlintVersion: String,
222→) {
223→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
224→        LOGGER.warn {
225→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
226→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
227→        }
228→    }

232→    val rule: Rule,
233→    val line: Int,

22→    ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,

25→     * [Rule.VisitorModifier]s.

27→    recreateRuleSorter: Boolean = false,
28→) {
29→    /**
30→     * The list of [ruleProvidersSorted] is sorted based on the [Rule.VisitorModifier] of the rules.

Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me now look at the existing format function more carefully and understand the flow:

1. The `format` function creates a `RuleExecutionContext` from the code
2. It runs all rules with `autoCorrect = true` in the first pass
3. If any violations were found (`tripped = true`), it does a second pass with `autoCorrect = false` to find remaining violations
4. It collects and reports all errors

The issue is asking me to:
- If format finds violations that can be autocorrected, rerun the format on the modified code
- Maximum 3 reruns to prevent infinite loops
- Return the lint violations from the last format run

Let me now implement this. The key change is in the `format` method of `KtLintRuleEngine`. I need to:

1. After the first format pass, extract the formatted code

 93→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 94→     */
 95→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97→
 98→        return this
 99→    }

101→    /**
102→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
103→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
104→     * silently overwritten.
105→     *
106→     * Example of usage:
107→     * ```
108→     *  val code =
109→     *      """
110→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
111→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
112→     *      """.trimIndent()
113→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
114→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
115→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
116→     * ```
117→     */
118→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
119→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
120→        code
121→            .split("\n")
122→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
123→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
124→            ?.let { index -> editorConfigProperties.add(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString()) }
125→            ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
126→
127→        return this
128→    }

293→    public companion object {
294→        /**
295→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
296→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules.
297→         */
298→        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
299→
300→        /**
301→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
302→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only
303→         * executed during the format phase of the test. This means that the unit test only has to check the lint
304→         * violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But the code is formatted by both the rule
305→         * and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule definitions.
306→         */
307→
308→        public fun assertThatRule(
309→            provider: () -> Rule,
310→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
311→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
312→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders, editorConfigProperties)
313→
314→        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(
315→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
316→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
317→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
318→            { code ->
319→                KtLintAssertThat(
320→                    ruleProvider = this,
321→                    code = code,
322→                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
323→                    editorConfigProperties = editorConfigProperties.toMutableSet(),
324→                )
325→            }
326→
327→        /**
328→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
329→         */
330→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
331→
332→        /**
333→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
334→         */
335→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
336→
337→        init {
338→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
339→             * val code =
340→             *     """
341→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
342→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
343→             *     """.trimIndent()
344→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
345→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
346→             */
347→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
348→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
349→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
350→            }
351→        }
352→    }
353→}

355→/**
356→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
357→ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
358→ * assertions operate on the same state.
359→ */
360→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
361→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
362→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
363→    private val code: Code,
364→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
365→    /**
366→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
367→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
368→     */
369→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
370→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
371→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
372→
373→    /**
374→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
375→     * KtLintAssertThat.
376→     *
377→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
378→     */
379→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
380→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
381→
382→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
383→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
384→
385→        assertAll(
386→            {
387→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
388→                    .describedAs(
389→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
390→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
391→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
392→                            } else {
393→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
394→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
395→                            },
396→                    )
397→                    .isEmpty()
398→            },
399→            {
400→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
401→                    .describedAs(
402→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
403→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
404→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
405→                            } else {
406→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
407→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
408→                            },
409→                    )
410→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
411→            },
412→        )
413→    }
414→
415→    /**
416→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
417→     */
418→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
419→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
420→
421→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
422→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '${ruleId.value}' while none were expected")
423→            .isEmpty()
424→
425→        return this
426→    }
427→
428→    /**
429→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
430→     *
431→     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly
432→     * formatted.
433→     *
434→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample
435→     * code, but the code is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each
436→     * other, a unit test cal still verify that code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not
437→     * violated.
438→     */
439→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
440→        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
441→            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
442→        }
443→
444→        val lintErrors = lint()
445→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
446→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
447→
448→        return this
449→    }
450→
451→    /**
452→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar coated version of
453→     * [hasLintViolation] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
454→     */
455→    public fun hasLintViolation(
456→        line: Int,
457→        col: Int,
458→        detail: String,
459→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =

467→
468→    /**
469→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar coated

472→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(

518→    /**

540→        line: Int,

552→    /**
553→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically

556→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {

559→        val actualLintViolationFields =

572→                .map {

578→                    )
579→                }

581→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)

604→                line = it.line,

611→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
612→        val ruleProviders =
613→            setOf(ruleProvider)
614→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
615→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
616→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
617→        val editorConfigOverride =
618→            editorConfigOverride
619→                .enableExperimentalRules()
620→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
621→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
622→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
623→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
624→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
625→        )
626→    }
627→
628→    private fun lint(): List<LintError> {
629→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
630→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
631→        return lintErrors
632→    }
633→
634→    private fun format(): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
635→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
636→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
637→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
638→    }

642→     */
643→    private data class LintViolationFields(
644→        val line: Int,
645→        val col: Int,
646→        val detail: String,
647→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
648→    )

651→internal class MissingEolMarker : RuntimeException(
652→    """
653→    The first line of the provide code sample should contain text '$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' which is provided by the
654→    constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
655→    '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
656→    """.trimIndent(),
657→)
658→
659→/**
660→ * Expectation of the [LintError]. Contrary to the [LintError] it does not contain the ruleId. The ruleId will be
661→ * derived from the rule for which the AssertThat was created.
662→ */
663→public data class LintViolation(
664→    val line: Int,
665→    val col: Int,
666→    val detail: String,
667→    val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean = true,
668→)
669→

134→

146→            var formatRunMutated = false

148→                .visitor()
149→                .invoke { rule ->
150→                    ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
151→                        tripped = true

177→                    }
178→                }

180→            if (!formatRunMutated) {

185→                        .visitor()
186→                        .invoke { rule ->
187→                            ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(
188→                                rule,
189→                                false,
190→                            ) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
191→                                val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
192→                                errors.add(
193→                                    Pair(
194→                                        LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
195→                                        // It is assumed that a rule only corrects an error after it has emitted an

197→                                        false,

201→                        }
202→                }

225→            } else {

227→                LOGGER.debug {

232→                val finalVisitorProvider = VisitorProvider(finalRuleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)

234→                    .visitor()
235→                    .invoke { rule ->

242→                                Pair(

248→                    }
249→            }

252→        errors
253→            .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
254→            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }

256→        if (!mutated) {

content.text:
All tests pass. Let me now summarize the changes I made:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
The order in which rules are executed can influence whether all format errors are resolved in a single pass. When rules have complex dependencies (via `Rule.VisitorModifier`), it becomes difficult to find a single execution order that always resolves all format errors in one trip. This is confusing for users.

### Solution
Modified the `format` method in `KtLintRuleEngine` to automatically repeat the format step when autocorrectable errors are found, up to a maximum of 3 runs (`MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE`). This ensures that errors introduced or revealed by one rule's corrections can be caught and fixed by subsequent runs.

### Files Changed

1. **`ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/api/Code.kt`**
   - Added an `internal fun copy(content: String)` method that creates a new `Code` instance with different content but preserving all other properties (fileName, filePath, script, isStdIn). This is needed to re-parse the formatted code while maintaining the file context for editor config resolution.

2. **`ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/api/KtLintRuleEngine.kt`**
   - Rewrote the `format` method to loop up to `MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE` (3) times:
     - Each iteration creates a fresh `RuleExecutionContext` from the (possibly modified) code
     - Runs all rules with `autoCorrect = true`
     - If no mutations occurred in the current run, breaks out of the loop (optionally doing a lint-only pass if violations were found)
     - If mutations occurred and more runs are allowed, re-parses the formatted output and continues the loop
     - On the last allowed run, does a final lint-only pass on the formatted result
   - Added `MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE = 3` constant to the companion object
   - The final formatted content is returned from `currentCode.content` which tracks the latest formatted version

### Key Design Decisions
- **Maximum 3 runs** prevents infinite loops from rules that might flip-flop
- **Early exit** when no mutations occur avoids unnecessary re-parsing
- **Preserving file context** via `Code.copy()` ensures editor config settings are maintained across runs
- **Error collection** across all runs ensures complete reporting

21→     */
22→    ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,

28→) {
29→    /**
30→     * The list of [ruleProvidersSorted] is sorted based on the [Rule.VisitorModifier] of the rules.
31→     */
32→    private val ruleProvidersSorted: List<RuleProvider> =
33→        if (recreateRuleSorter) {
34→            RuleProviderSorter()
35→        } else {
36→            RULE_PROVIDER_SORTER
37→        }.getSortedRuleProviders(ruleProviders)

39→    internal fun visitor(): ((rule: Rule) -> Unit) -> Unit {
40→        if (ruleProvidersSorted.isEmpty()) {
41→            LOGGER.debug { "Skipping file as no enabled rules are found to be executed" }
42→            return { _ -> }
43→        }
44→        return { visit ->
45→            ruleProvidersSorted.forEach {
46→                visit(it.createNewRuleInstance())

48→        }
49→    }

 72→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 73→                """.trimIndent(),
 74→                e.cause,
 75→            )
 76→        }
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 80→        node: ASTNode,
 81→        rule: Rule,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        /**
 86→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 87→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 88→         */
 89→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 91→            try {
 92→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 93→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→                }
 95→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 96→                    node
 97→                        .getChildren(null)
 98→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 99→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
100→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
101→                                    childNode,
102→                                    rule,
103→                                    autoCorrect,
104→                                    emit,
105→                                )
106→                            }
107→                        }
108→                }
109→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
110→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
111→                }
112→            } catch (e: Exception) {
113→                if (autoCorrect) {
114→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
115→                    // original AST
116→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
117→                        rule,
118→                        0,
119→                        0,
120→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
121→                        e.cause ?: e,
122→                    )
123→                } else {
124→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
125→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
126→                        rule,
127→                        line,
128→                        col,
129→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
130→                        e.cause ?: e,
131→                    )
132→                }
133→            }
134→        }
135→    }
136→
137→    companion object {
138→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
139→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
140→            code: Code,
141→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
142→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine)
143→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
144→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
145→
146→            val psiFileName =
147→                code
148→                    .filePath
149→                    ?.pathString
150→                    ?: if (code.script) {
151→                        "File.kts"
152→                    } else {
153→                        "File.kt"
154→                    }
155→            val psiFile =
156→                psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
157→                    psiFileName,
158→                    KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
159→                    normalizedText,
160→                ) as KtFile
161→            psiFile
162→                .findErrorElement()
163→                ?.let { errorElement ->
164→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
165→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
166→                }
167→
168→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
169→
170→            val editorConfig =
171→                ktLintRuleEngine
172→                    .editorConfigLoader
173→                    .load(code.filePath)
174→                    .also {
175→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
176→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
177→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
178→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
179→                    }
180→
181→            val ruleProviders =
182→                ktLintRuleEngine
183→                    .ruleProviders(
184→                        RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
185→                        RunAfterRuleFilter(),
186→                    )
187→
188→            return RuleExecutionContext(
189→                code,
190→                rootNode,
191→                ruleProviders,
192→                editorConfig,
193→                positionInTextLocator,
194→            )
195→        }
196→
197→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
198→            return text
199→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
200→                .replace("\r", "\n")
201→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
202→        }
203→
204→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
205→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
206→                return this
207→            }
208→            this.children.forEach { child ->
209→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
210→                if (errorElement != null) {
211→                    return errorElement
212→                }
213→            }
214→            return null
215→        }
216→    }
217→}

226→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
227→        }

100→    ) {

141→        val visitorProvider = VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)

144→            .invoke { rule ->
145→                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
146→                    tripped = true
147→                    if (canBeAutoCorrected) {

174→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
175→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
176→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
177→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
178→     *
179→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProvider] when only a singe provider of a rule is to be added.
180→     */
181→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProviders(vararg providers: (() -> Rule)): KtLintAssertThat {
182→        additionalRuleProviders.addAll(
183→            providers.map { RuleProvider(it) },
184→        )
185→
186→        return this
187→    }

293→    public companion object {
294→        /**
295→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
296→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules.
297→         */
298→        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
299→
300→        /**
301→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
302→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only
303→         * executed during the format phase of the test. This means that the unit test only has to check the lint
304→         * violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But the code is formatted by both the rule
305→         * and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule definitions.
306→         */
307→
308→        public fun assertThatRule(
309→            provider: () -> Rule,
310→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
311→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
312→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders, editorConfigProperties)
313→
314→        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(
315→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
316→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
317→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
318→            { code ->
319→                KtLintAssertThat(
320→                    ruleProvider = this,
321→                    code = code,
322→                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
323→                    editorConfigProperties = editorConfigProperties.toMutableSet(),
324→                )
325→            }
326→
327→        /**
328→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
329→         */
330→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
331→
332→        /**
333→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
334→         */
335→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
336→
337→        init {
338→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
339→             * val code =
340→             *     """
341→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
342→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
343→             *     """.trimIndent()
344→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
345→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
346→             */
347→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
348→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
349→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
350→            }
351→        }
352→    }
353→}

355→/**
356→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
357→ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
358→ * assertions operate on the same state.
359→ */
360→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
361→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
362→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
363→    private val code: Code,
364→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
365→    /**
366→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
367→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
368→     */
369→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
370→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
371→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
372→
373→    /**
374→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
375→     * KtLintAssertThat.
376→     *
377→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
378→     */
379→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
380→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
381→
382→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
383→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
384→
385→        assertAll(
386→            {
387→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
388→                    .describedAs(
389→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
390→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
391→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
392→                            } else {
393→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
394→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
395→                            },
396→                    )
397→                    .isEmpty()
398→            },
399→            {
400→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
401→                    .describedAs(
402→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
403→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
404→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
405→                            } else {
406→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
407→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
408→                            },
409→                    )
410→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
411→            },
412→        )
413→    }
414→
415→    /**
416→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
417→     */
418→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
419→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
420→
421→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
422→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '${ruleId.value}' while none were expected")
423→            .isEmpty()
424→
425→        return this
426→    }
427→
428→    /**
429→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
430→     *
431→     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly
432→     * formatted.
433→     *
434→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample
435→     * code, but the code is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each
436→     * other, a unit test cal still verify that code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not
437→     * violated.
438→     */
439→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
440→        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
441→            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
442→        }
443→
444→        val lintErrors = lint()
445→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
446→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
447→
448→        return this
449→    }
450→
451→    /**
452→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar coated version of
453→     * [hasLintViolation] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
454→     */
455→    public fun hasLintViolation(
456→        line: Int,
457→        col: Int,
458→        detail: String,
459→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
460→        hasLintViolations(
461→            LintViolation(
462→                line = line,
463→                col = col,
464→                detail = detail,
465→            ),
466→        )
467→
468→    /**
469→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar coated
470→     * version of [hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
471→     */
472→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
473→        line: Int,
474→        col: Int,
475→        detail: String,
476→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
477→        hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(
478→            LintViolation(
479→                line = line,
480→                col = col,
481→                detail = detail,
482→            ),
483→        )
484→
485→    /**
486→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected.
487→     */
488→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
489→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
490→
491→        val actualLintViolationFields =
492→            lint()
493→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
494→                .toLintViolationsFields()
495→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
496→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
497→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields())
498→        return this
499→    }
500→
501→    /**
502→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by additional rules and which can be
503→     * automatically corrected.
504→     */
505→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
506→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
507→
508→        val actualLintViolationFields =
509→            lint()
510→                .filterAdditionalRulesOnly()
511→                .toLintViolationsFields()
512→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
513→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
514→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields())
515→        return this
516→    }
517→
518→    /**
519→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
520→     */
521→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
522→        check(formattedCode != code.content) {
523→            "Use '.hasNoLintViolations()' instead of '.isFormattedAs(<original code>)'"
524→        }
525→
526→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
527→        val (actualFormattedCode, _) = format()
528→
529→        assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
530→            .describedAs("Code is formatted as")
531→            .isEqualTo(formattedCode)
532→
533→        return this
534→    }
535→
536→    /**
537→     * Asserts that the code contains the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
538→     */
539→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
540→        line: Int,
541→        col: Int,
542→        detail: String,
543→    ): Unit =
544→        hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(
545→            LintViolation(
546→                line = line,
547→                col = col,
548→                detail = detail,
549→            ),
550→        )
551→
552→    /**
553→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically
554→     * corrected.
555→     */
556→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {
557→        check(expectedLintViolations.isNotEmpty())
558→
559→        val actualLintViolationFields =
560→            lint()
561→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
562→                .map {
563→                    LintViolationFields(
564→                        line = it.line,
565→                        col = it.col,
566→                        detail = it.detail,
567→                        canBeAutoCorrected = false,
568→                    )
569→                }.toTypedArray()
570→        val expectedLintViolationFields =
571→            expectedLintViolations
572→                .map {
573→                    LintViolationFields(
574→                        line = it.line,
575→                        col = it.col,
576→                        detail = it.detail,
577→                        canBeAutoCorrected = false,
578→                    )
579→                }
580→
581→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
582→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can not be automatically corrected")
583→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedLintViolationFields.toTypedArray())
584→    }

586→    private fun Array<out LintViolation>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> {
587→        return map {
588→            LintViolationFields(
589→                line = it.line,
590→                col = it.col,
591→                detail = it.detail,
592→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
593→            )
594→        }.toTypedArray()
595→    }

597→    private fun List<LintError>.filterAdditionalRulesOnly() = filter { it.ruleId != ruleId }
598→
599→    private fun List<LintError>.filterCurrentRuleOnly() = filter { it.ruleId == ruleId }
600→
601→    private fun List<LintError>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> =
602→        map {
603→            LintViolationFields(
604→                line = it.line,
605→                col = it.col,
606→                detail = it.detail,
607→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
608→            )
609→        }.toTypedArray()

611→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
612→        val ruleProviders =
613→            setOf(ruleProvider)
614→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
615→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
616→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
617→        val editorConfigOverride =
618→            editorConfigOverride
619→                .enableExperimentalRules()
620→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
621→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
622→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
623→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
624→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
625→        )
626→    }
627→
628→    private fun lint(): List<LintError> {
629→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
630→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
631→        return lintErrors
632→    }
633→
634→    private fun format(): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
635→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
636→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
637→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
638→    }
639→
640→    /* Representation of the field of the [LintError] that should be identical. Note that no comparison can be made
641→     * against the original [LintError] as the [canBeAutoCorrected] flag is excluded from the hashcode.
642→     */
643→    private data class LintViolationFields(
644→        val line: Int,
645→        val col: Int,
646→        val detail: String,
647→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
648→    )
649→}

651→internal class MissingEolMarker : RuntimeException(
652→    """
653→    The first line of the provide code sample should contain text '$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' which is provided by the
654→    constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
655→    '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
656→    """.trimIndent(),
657→)
658→
659→/**
660→ * Expectation of the [LintError]. Contrary to the [LintError] it does not contain the ruleId. The ruleId will be
661→ * derived from the rule for which the AssertThat was created.
662→ */
663→public data class LintViolation(
664→    val line: Int,
665→    val col: Int,
666→    val detail: String,
667→    val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean = true,
668→)
669→
670→/**
671→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
672→ * provided.
673→ */
674→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
675→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =
676→        ruleProviders
677→            .asSequence()
678→            .map { ruleProvider ->
679→                ruleProvider
680→                    .createNewRuleInstance()
681→                    .ruleId
682→                    .ruleSetId
683→                    .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
684→            }.distinct()
685→            .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
686→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
687→            .toList()
688→            .toTypedArray()
689→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
690→}
691→
692→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
693→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)

 50→    text: String,
 51→    filePath: String? = null,
 52→    editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 53→): List<LintError> {
 54→    val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
 55→    val code =
 56→        if (filePath != null) {
 57→            Code.fromFile(KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath).toFile())
 58→        } else {
 59→            Code.fromSnippet(text)
 60→        }
 61→    KtLintRuleEngine(
 62→        ruleProviders = this,
 63→        editorConfigOverride =
 64→            editorConfigOverride
 65→                .enableExperimentalRules()
 66→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(this),
 67→        fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
 68→    ).lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
 69→    return lintErrors
 70→}
 71→
 72→/**
 73→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
 74→ * provided.
 75→ */
 76→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
 77→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =
 78→        ruleProviders
 79→            .asSequence()
 80→            .map { ruleProvider ->
 81→                ruleProvider
 82→                    .createNewRuleInstance()
 83→                    .ruleId
 84→                    .ruleSetId
 85→                    .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
 86→            }.distinct()
 87→            .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
 88→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
 89→            .toList()
 90→            .toTypedArray()
 91→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
 92→}
 93→
 94→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
 95→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
 96→
 97→/**
 98→ * Execute [KtLintRuleEngine.format] on given code snippet. To test a kotlin script file, provide a filepath ending with
 99→ * ".kts". For each invocation of this method, a fresh instance of the [KtLintRuleEngine] is instantiated for the given
100→ * set of rules.
101→ * This method is intended to be executed in a unit test environment only. If the project that is containing the unit
102→ * test contains an '.editorconfig' file, then it will be ignored entirely. Provide '.editorconfig' properties that have
103→ * to be applied on the code snippet via [editorConfigOverride].
104→ */
105→@Deprecated("Marked for removal in Ktlint 0.50. Raise an issue if you actually are using this function.")
106→public fun Set<RuleProvider>.format(
107→    text: String,
108→    filePath: String?,
109→    editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
110→): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
111→    val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
112→    val code =
113→        if (filePath != null) {
114→            Code.fromFile(KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath).toFile())
115→        } else {
116→            Code.fromSnippet(text)
117→        }
118→    val formattedCode =
119→        KtLintRuleEngine(
120→            ruleProviders = this,
121→            editorConfigOverride =
122→                editorConfigOverride
123→                    .enableExperimentalRules()
124→                    .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(this),
125→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
126→        ).format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
127→    return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
128→}
129→
130→// The execution of the unit tests may not be affected by the ".editorconfig" configuration of the Ktlint project itself. So each unit test
131→// is to be run on a KtlintTestFileSystem not having any ".editorconfig" files. This variable should not be exposed, as unit tests should
132→// not be allowed to write any content on the file system.
133→// As this FileSystem is not modified it is not considered to be a problem that the file system is not closed after each unit test.
134→private val KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM = KtlintTestFileSystem()

 34→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
