 36→public class KtLintRuleEngine(
 37→    /**
 38→     * The set of [RuleProvider]s to be invoked by the [KtLintRuleEngine]. A [RuleProvider] is able to create a new instance of a [Rule] so
 39→     * that it can keep internal state and be called thread-safe manner
 40→     */
 41→    public val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
 42→    /**
 43→     * The default values for `.editorconfig` properties which are not set explicitly in any '.editorconfig' file located on the path of the
 44→     * file which is processed with the [KtLintRuleEngine]. If a property is set in [editorConfigDefaults] this takes precedence above the
 45→     * default values defined in the KtLint project.
 46→     */
 47→    public val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 48→    /**
 49→     * Override values for `.editorconfig` properties. If a property is set in [editorConfigOverride] it takes precedence above the same
 50→     * property being set in any other way.
 51→     */
 52→    public val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 53→    /**
 54→     * Temporary flag to indicate that kotlin embeddable compiler is to be executed with (default) or without the extension point
 55→     * 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler'. This extension point is not (yet) supported in the preview of Kotlin 1.9. Some
 56→     * rules might no longer work and throw exceptions at runtime.
 57→     * It is unclear whether the extension point will be supported. Disabling this flag on the Kotlin 1.8 compiler has the same effect. As
 58→     * of that it can be used to assess the impact, and to fix rules before release of the Ktlint version which will be based on Kotlin 1.9.
 59→     */
 60→    public val enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint: Boolean = true,
 61→    /**
 62→     * **For internal use only**: indicates that linting was invoked from KtLint CLI tool. It enables some internals workarounds for Kotlin
 63→     * Compiler initialization. This property is likely to be removed in any of next versions without further notice.
 64→     */
 65→    public val isInvokedFromCli: Boolean = false,
 66→    /**
 67→     * The [FileSystem] to be used. This property is primarily intended to be used in unit tests. By specifying an alternative [FileSystem]
 68→     * the unit test gains control on whether the [EditorConfigLoader] should or should not read specific ".editorconfig" files. For
 69→     * example, it is considered unwanted that a unit test is influenced by the ".editorconfig" file of the project in which the unit test
 70→     * is included.
 71→     */
 72→    public val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 73→) {
 74→    init {
 75→        require(ruleProviders.any()) {
 76→            "A non-empty set of 'ruleProviders' need to be provided"
 77→        }
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    internal val editorConfigLoaderEc4j = EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
 81→
 82→    internal val editorConfigLoader =
 83→        EditorConfigLoader(
 84→            fileSystem,
 85→            editorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 86→            editorConfigDefaults,
 87→            editorConfigOverride,
 88→        )
 89→
 90→    /**
 91→     * Check the [code] for lint errors. If [code] is path as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on the path to file are taken
 92→     * into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
 93→     *
 94→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
 95→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
 96→     */
 97→    public fun lint(
 98→        code: Code,
 99→        callback: (LintError) -> Unit = { },
100→    ) {
101→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
102→
103→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
104→        val errors = mutableListOf<LintError>()
105→
106→        VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
107→            .visitor()
108→            .invoke { rule ->
109→                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, false) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
110→                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
111→                    errors.add(LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected))
112→                }
113→            }
114→
115→        errors
116→            .sortedWith { l, r -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
117→            .forEach { e -> callback(e) }
118→
119→        LOGGER.debug { "Finished with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
120→    }
121→
122→    /**
123→     * Fix style violations in [code] for lint errors when possible. If [code] is passed as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on
124→     * the path are taken into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
125→     *
126→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
127→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
128→     */
129→    public fun format(
130→        code: Code,
131→        callback: (LintError, Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _ -> },
132→    ): String {
133→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
134→
135→        val hasUTF8BOM = code.content.startsWith(UTF8_BOM)
136→
137→        var mutated = false
138→        var currentCode = code
139→        val errors = mutableSetOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
140→
141→        for (run in 1..MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE) {
142→            val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, currentCode)
143→            val visitorProvider = VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
144→
145→            var tripped = false
146→            var formatRunMutated = false
147→            visitorProvider
148→                .visitor()
149→                .invoke { rule ->
150→                    ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
151→                        tripped = true
152→                        if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
153→                            mutated = true
154→                            formatRunMutated = true
155→                            /**
156→                             * Rebuild the suppression locator after each change in the AST as the offsets of the
157→                             * suppression hints might have changed.
158→                             */
159→                            ruleExecutionContext.rebuildSuppressionLocator()
160→                        }
161→                        val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
162→                        errors.add(
163→                            Pair(
164→                                LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
165→                                // It is assumed that a rule that emits that an error can be autocorrected, also
166→                                // does correct the error.
167→                                canBeAutoCorrected,
168→                            ),
169→                        )
170→                        // In trace mode report the violation immediately. The order in which violations are actually found might be
171→                        // different from the order in which they are reported. For debugging purposes it can be helpful to know the
172→                        // exact order in which violations are being solved.
173→                        LOGGER.trace {
174→                            "Format violation (run $run): ${currentCode.fileNameOrStdin()}:$line:$col: $errorMessage (${rule.ruleId})" +
175→                                canBeAutoCorrected.ifFalse { " [cannot be autocorrected]" }
176→                        }
177→                    }
178→                }
179→
180→            if (!formatRunMutated) {
181→                // No changes were made in this run, so no need to rerun
182→                if (tripped) {
183→                    // Report remaining lint violations that could not be autocorrected
184→                    visitorProvider
185→                        .visitor()
186→                        .invoke { rule ->
187→                            ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(
188→                                rule,
189→                                false,
190→                            ) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
191→                                val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
192→                                errors.add(
193→                                    Pair(
194→                                        LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
195→                                        // It is assumed that a rule only corrects an error after it has emitted an
196→                                        // error and indicating that it actually can be autocorrected.
197→                                        false,
198→                                    ),
199→                                )
200→                            }
201→                        }
202→                }
203→                break
204→            }
205→
206→            // Extract the formatted code from the AST to use as input for the next run
207→            val formattedCode =
208→                ruleExecutionContext
209→                    .rootNode
210→                    .text
211→                    .replace("\n", ruleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(currentCode.content))
212→            val formattedContent =
213→                if (hasUTF8BOM) {
214→                    UTF8_BOM + formattedCode
215→                } else {
216→                    formattedCode
217→                }
218→
219→            if (run < MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE) {
220→                // Prepare code for next run by re-parsing the formatted output
221→                currentCode = currentCode.copy(content = formattedContent)
222→                LOGGER.debug {
223→                    "Format run $run of file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}' modified the code. Rerunning format."
224→                }
225→            } else {
226→                // Last allowed run - do the lint-only check to find remaining errors
227→                LOGGER.debug {
228→                    "Format run $run of file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}' modified the code. Max $MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE format runs reached."
229→                }
230→                currentCode = currentCode.copy(content = formattedContent)
231→                val finalRuleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, currentCode)
232→                val finalVisitorProvider = VisitorProvider(finalRuleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
233→                finalVisitorProvider
234→                    .visitor()
235→                    .invoke { rule ->
236→                        finalRuleExecutionContext.executeRule(
237→                            rule,
238→                            false,
239→                        ) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
240→                            val (line, col) = finalRuleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
241→                            errors.add(
242→                                Pair(
243→                                    LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
244→                                    false,
245→                                ),
246→                            )
247→                        }
248→                    }
249→            }
250→        }
251→
252→        errors
253→            .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
254→            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
255→
256→        if (!mutated) {
257→            return code.content
258→        }
259→
260→        return currentCode.content.also {
261→            LOGGER.debug("Finished with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'")
262→        }
263→    }
264→
265→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
266→        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
267→        return when {
268→            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
269→            else -> "\n"
270→        }
271→    }
272→
273→    /**
274→     * Generates Kotlin `.editorconfig` file section content based on a path to a file or directory. Given that path, all '.editorconfig'
275→     * files on that path are taken into account to determine the values of properties which are already used.
276→     *
277→     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
278→     * ```properties
279→     * final-newline=true
280→     * indent-size=4
281→     * ```
282→     */
283→    public fun generateKotlinEditorConfigSection(filePath: Path): String {
284→        val codeStyle =
285→            editorConfigOverride
286→                .properties[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
287→                ?.parsed
288→                ?.safeAs<CodeStyleValue>()
289→                ?: CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
290→        val rules =
291→            ruleProviders
292→                .map { it.createNewRuleInstance() }
293→                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
294→                .toSet()
295→        return EditorConfigGenerator(fileSystem, editorConfigLoaderEc4j)
296→            .generateEditorconfig(
297→                rules,
298→                codeStyle,
299→                filePath,
300→            )
301→    }
302→
303→    /**
304→     * Reduce memory usage by cleaning internal caches.
305→     */
306→    public fun trimMemory() {
307→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.clear()
308→    }
309→
310→    /**
311→     * Get the list of files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting the given file or directory.
312→     * The API consumer can use this list to observe changes in '.editorconfig' files. Whenever such a change is
313→     * observed, the API consumer should call [reloadEditorConfigFile].
314→     * To avoid unnecessary access to the file system, it is best to call this method only once for the root of the
315→     * project which is to be [lint] or [format].
316→     */
317→    public fun editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path> = EditorConfigFinder(editorConfigLoaderEc4j).findEditorConfigs(path)
318→
319→    /**
320→     * Reloads an '.editorconfig' file given that it is currently loaded into the KtLint cache. This method is intended
321→     * to be called by the API consumer when it is aware of changes in the '.editorconfig' file that should be taken
322→     * into account with next calls to [lint] and/or [format]. See [editorConfigFilePaths] to get the list of
323→     * '.editorconfig' files which need to be observed.
324→     */
325→    public fun reloadEditorConfigFile(path: Path) {
326→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.reloadIfExists(
327→            Resource.Resources.ofPath(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8),
328→        )
329→    }
330→
331→    public fun transformToAst(code: Code): FileASTNode = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code).rootNode
332→
333→    public companion object {
334→        internal const val UTF8_BOM = "\uFEFF"
335→
336→        public const val STDIN_FILE: String = "<stdin>"
337→
338→        /**
339→         * Maximum number of times the format is to be repeated when autocorrectable errors are found. This is to prevent an infinite loop
340→         * when a set of rules keeps on changing the code back and forth.
341→         */
342→        internal const val MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE = 3
343→    }
344→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine.Companion.STDIN_FILE
 4→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.konan.file.file
 5→import java.io.File
 6→import java.nio.file.Path
 7→import kotlin.io.path.pathString
 8→
 9→/**
10→ * A representation of a block of code. Use one of the factory methods [fromFile], [fromPath], [fromSnippet] or [fromStdin] to instantiate.
11→ */
12→public class Code private constructor(
13→    public val content: String,
14→    public val fileName: String?,
15→    public val filePath: Path?,
16→    public val script: Boolean,
17→    public val isStdIn: Boolean,
18→) {
19→    public fun fileNameOrStdin(): String =
20→        if (isStdIn) {
21→            STDIN_FILE
22→        } else {
23→            fileName.orEmpty()
24→        }
25→
26→    internal fun copy(content: String): Code =
27→        Code(
28→            content = content,
29→            fileName = fileName,
30→            filePath = filePath,
31→            script = script,
32→            isStdIn = isStdIn,
33→        )
34→
35→    public companion object {
36→        /**
37→         * Create [Code] from a [file] containing valid Kotlin code or script. The '.editorconfig' files on the path to [file] are taken
38→         * into account.
39→         */
40→        public fun fromFile(file: File): Code =
41→            Code(
42→                content = file.readText(),
43→                fileName = file.name,
44→                filePath = file.toPath(),
45→                script = file.name.endsWith(".kts", ignoreCase = true),
46→                isStdIn = false,
47→            )
48→
49→        /**
50→         * Create [Code] from a [path] to a file containing valid Kotlin code or script. The '.editorconfig' files on the path to [file] are
51→         * taken into account. This method is intended to be used in unit tests. In order to work with the Ktlint test file system it needs
52→         * to make additional call to get the file system which makes it slower compared to [fromFile]. Prefer to use [fromFile].
53→         */
54→        public fun fromPath(path: Path): Code {
55→            // Resolve the file based on the file system of the original path given.
56→            val file =
57→                path
58→                    .fileSystem
59→                    .file(path.pathString)
60→            return Code(
61→                content = file.readStrings().joinToString(separator = "\n"),
62→                fileName = file.name,
63→                filePath = path,
64→                script = file.name.endsWith(".kts", ignoreCase = true),
65→                isStdIn = false,
66→            )
67→        }
68→
69→        /**
70→         * The [content] represent a valid piece of Kotlin code or Kotlin script. The '.editorconfig' files on the filesystem are ignored as
71→         * the snippet is not associated with a file path. Use [Code.fromFile] for scanning a file while at the same time respecting the
72→         * '.editorconfig' files on the path to the file.
73→         */
74→        public fun fromSnippet(
75→            content: String,
76→            script: Boolean = false,
77→        ): Code =
78→            Code(
79→                content = content,
80→                filePath = null,
81→                fileName = null,
82→                script = script,
83→                isStdIn = true,
84→            )
85→
86→        /**
87→         * Create [Code] by reading the snippet from 'stdin'. No '.editorconfig' are taken into account.  The '.editorconfig' files on the
88→         * filesystem are ignored as the snippet is not associated with a file path. Use [Code.fromFile] for scanning a file while at the
89→         * same time respecting the '.editorconfig' files on the path to the file.
90→         */
91→        public fun fromStdin(): Code = fromSnippet(String(System.`in`.readBytes()))
92→    }
93→}
